287	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
288	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
289	
290	\begin{definition}
291	
292	\leanok
293	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
294	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
295	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
296	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
297	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
298	  \[
299	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
300	  \]
301	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
302	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
303	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
304	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
305	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
306	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
307	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
308	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
309	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
310	  canonical compatibility
311	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
312	     \;\cong\;
313	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
314	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
315	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
316	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
317	
318	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
319	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
320	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
321	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
322	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
323	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
324	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
325	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
326	\end{definition}
327	
328	\begin{lemma}
329	
330	\leanok
331	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
332	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
334	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
335	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
336	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
337	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
338	  \[
339	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
340	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
342	  \]
343	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
344	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
345	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
346	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
347	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
348	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
349	  \[
350	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
351	    \qquad
352	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
353	  \]
354	  the associator
355	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
356	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
357	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
358	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
359	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
360	\end{lemma}
361	
362	\begin{proof}
363	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
364	  \leanok
365	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
366	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
367	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
368	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
369	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
370	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
371	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
372	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
373	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
374	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
375	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
376	\end{proof}
377	
378	\begin{remark}
379	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
380	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
381	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
382	        lem:whisker_of_W}
383	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
384	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
385	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
386	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
387	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
388	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
389	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
390	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
391	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
392	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
393	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
394	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
395	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
396	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
397	
398	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
399	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
400	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
401	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
402	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
403	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
404	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
405	  fixed-base presheaves in
406	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
407	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
408	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
409	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
410	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
411	  it.
412	
413	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
414	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
415	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
416	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
417	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
418	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
419	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
420	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
421	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
422	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
423	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
424	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
425	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
426	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
427	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
428	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
429	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
430	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
431	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
432	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
433	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
434	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
435	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
436	\end{remark}
437	
438	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
439	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
440	
441	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
442	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
443	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
444	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
445	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
446	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
447	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
448	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
450	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
451	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
452	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
453	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
454	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
455	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
456	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
457	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
458	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
459	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
460	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
461	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
462	to the off-path route~(e).
463	
464	\begin{lemma}
465	
466	\leanok
467	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
468	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
469	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
470	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
471	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
472	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
473	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
474	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
475	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
476	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
477	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
478	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
479	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
480	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
481	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
482	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
483	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
484	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
485	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
486	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
487	  \[
488	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
489	      \;\;:=\;\;
490	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
491	  \]
492	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
493	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
494	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
495	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
496	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
497	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
498	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
499	\end{lemma}
500	
501	\begin{proof}
502	
503	\leanok
504	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
505	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
506	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
507	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
508	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
509	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
510	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
511	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
512	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
513	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
514	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
515	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
516	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
517	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
518	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
519	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
520	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
521	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
522	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
523	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
524	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
525	
526	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
527	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
528	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
529	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
530	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
531	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
532	\end{proof}
533	
534	\begin{lemma}
535	
536	\leanok
537	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
538	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
539	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
540	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
541	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
542	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
543	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
544	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
545	  morphism
546	  \[
547	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
548	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
549	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
550	  \]
551	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
552	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
553	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
554	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
555	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
556	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
557	\end{lemma}
558	
559	\begin{proof}
560	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
561	  \leanok
562	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
563	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
564	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
565	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
566	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
567	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
568	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
569	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
570	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
571	  isomorphism}
572	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
573	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
574	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
575	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
576	  therefore extension of scalars along
577	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
578	
579	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
580	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
581	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
582	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
583	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
584	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
585	  the abstract pullback functor.
586	
587	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
588	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
589	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
590	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
591	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
592	   \;\cong\;
593	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
594	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
595	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
596	  comparison
597	  \[
598	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
599	      \;\longrightarrow\;
600	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
601	      \;\otimes\;
602	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
603	  \]
604	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
605	
606	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
607	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
608	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
609	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
610	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
611	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
612	  % "\begin{lemma}
613	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
614	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
615	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
616	  % \begin{enumerate}
617	  % \item The functor
618	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
619	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
620	  % \item The functor
621	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
622	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
623	  %  [\dots]
624	  % \end{enumerate}
625	  % \end{lemma}
626	  %  [\dots]
627	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
628	  % of functors, see
629	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
630	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
631	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
632	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
633	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
634	
635	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
636	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
637	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
638	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
639	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
640	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
641	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
642	
643	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
644	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
645	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
646	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
647	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
648	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
649	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
650	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
651	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
652	  \otimes_S N)\).
653	
654	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
655	  \[
656	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
657	  \]
658	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
659	  presheaf-level adjunction
660	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
661	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
662	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
663	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
664	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
665	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
666	  composing the resulting comparison with
667	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
668	
669	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
670	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
671	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
672	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
673	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
674	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
675	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
676	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
677	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
678	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
679	
680	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
681	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
682	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
683	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
684	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
685	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
686	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
687	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
688	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
689	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
690	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
691	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
692	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
693	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
694	\end{proof}
695	
696	\begin{lemma}
697	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
698	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
699	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
700	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
701	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
702	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
703	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
704	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
705	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
706	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
707	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
708	
709	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
710	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
711	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
712	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
713	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
714	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
715	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
716	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
717	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
718	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
719	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
720	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
721	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
722	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
723	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
724	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
725	
726	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
727	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
728	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
729	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
730	  restriction-of-scalars functor
731	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
732	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
733	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
734	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
735	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
736	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
737	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
738	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
739	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
740	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
741	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
742	  morphism but is not syntactically a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).
743	
744	  Together H1 and H2 are the two Mathlib-absent ingredients the linchpin needed; they
745	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
746	\end{lemma}
747	
748	\begin{lemma}
749	
750	\leanok
751	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
752	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
753	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
754	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
755	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
756	  product: the canonical map
757	  \[
758	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
759	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
760	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
761	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
762	  \]
763	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
764	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
765	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
766	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
767	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
768	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
769	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
770	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
771	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
772	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
773	\end{lemma}
774	
775	\begin{lemma}
776	
777	[Strong-monoidal upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along a ring isomorphism (ModuleCat-level H2 core)]
778	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}
779	  \lean{restrictScalars_isIso_μ, restrictScalars_isIso_ε,
780	        restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv,
781	        restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective,
782	        restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective}

732	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
733	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
734	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
735	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
736	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
737	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
738	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
739	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
740	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
741	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
742	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
743	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
744	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
745	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
746	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
747	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
748	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
749	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
750	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
751	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
752	      intro z
753	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
754	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
755	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
756	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
757	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
758	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
759	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
760	
761	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
762	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
763	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
764	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
765	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
766	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
767	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
768	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
769	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
770	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
771	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
772	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
773	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
774	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
775	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
776	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
777	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
778	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
779	
780	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
781	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
782	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
783	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
784	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
785	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
786	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
787	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
788	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
789	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
790	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
791	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
792	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
793	
794	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
795	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
796	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
797	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
798	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
799	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
800	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
801	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
802	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
803	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
804	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
805	
806	end StalkLinearMap

810	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
811	
812	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
813	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
814	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
815	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
816	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
817	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
818	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
819	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
820	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
821	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
822	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
823	
824	namespace PresheafOfModules

831	
832	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
833	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}

836	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
837	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
838	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
839	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
840	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
841	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
842	  app M :=
843	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
844	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
845	        ext x
846	        dsimp
847	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
848	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
849	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
850	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
851	    ext U x
852	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
853	
854	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
855	    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
856	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl

862	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
863	
864	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
865	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}

867	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of

869	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the

872	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
873	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
874	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
875	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
876	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
877	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)

879	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
880	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
881	    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl

883	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
884	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
885	    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl

891	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD

893	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
894	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
895	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
896	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
897	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
898	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
899	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
900	
901	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
902	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
903	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
904	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
905	sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
906	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
907	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
908	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
909	  unit :=
910	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
911	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
912	  counit :=
913	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
914	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
915	  left_triangle_components X := by
916	    ext U x
917	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
918	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
919	    dsimp only [id_obj]
920	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
921	    simp
922	  right_triangle_components X := by
923	    ext U x
924	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
925	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
926	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
927	    simp
928	
929	end PushforwardAdj

937	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
938	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
939	naturality of the given morphism). -/
940	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
941	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
942	  haveI := h
943	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
944	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
945	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
946	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
947	
948	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
949	
950	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
951	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
952	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
953	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
954	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
955	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
956	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
957	@[implicit_reducible]
958	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
959	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
960	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
961	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
962	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
963	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
964	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
965	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
966	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
967	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
968	
969	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars

979	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
980	
981	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
982	
983	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
984	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
985	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
986	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
987	
988	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
989	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
990	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
991	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
992	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
993	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
994	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
995	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
996	
997	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
998	
999	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1000	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1001	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1002	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1003	
1004	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
1005	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
1006	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1007	sheafification. -/
1008	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1009	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1010	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1011	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1012	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1013	
1014	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1015	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1016	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1017	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1018	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1019	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1020	law is definitional. -/
1021	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1022	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1023	
1024	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1025	
1026	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1027	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1028	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1029	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1030	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1031	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1032	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1033	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1034	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1035	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1036	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1037	
1038	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1039	
1040	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1041	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1042	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1043	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1044	
1045	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1046	
1047	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1048	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1049	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1050	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1051	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1052	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1053	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1054	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1055	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1056	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1057	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1058	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1059	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1060	
1061	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1062	sheaf.**
1063	
1064	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1065	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1066	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1067	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1068	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1069	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1070	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1071	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1072	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1073	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1074	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1075	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1076	
1077	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1078	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1079	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
1080	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
1081	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
1082	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1083	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
1084	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1085	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1086	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1087	
1088	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1089	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
1090	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
1091	sheafification counit. -/
1092	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1093	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
1094	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1095	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1096	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1097	
1098	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
1099	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
1100	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val

1102	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
1103	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
1104	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso

1106	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)

1556	[Inverse of an invertible module]
1557	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
1558	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
1559	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
1560	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1561	  % NOTE: ON the critical path under the PRIMARY locally-trivial group law (it
1562	  % supplies inverses to lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup, in whose uses it sits).
1563	  % Only under the route-(e) fallback is it dispensable: there the group law uses
1564	  % the tensor-invertibility predicate IsInvertible (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible),
1565	  % which carries the inverse existentially, so no separate ``a tensor inverse
1566	  % exists'' lemma is required.
1567	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
1568	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2: the dual of an
1569	  % invertible is invertible and the contraction map is an isomorphism) +
1570	  % Definition~definition-pic (the Picard group as the abelian group of
1571	  % iso-classes of invertibles)
1572	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200--L4213 and L4350--L4357).
1573	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1574	  % "\begin{lemma}
1575	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
1576	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1577	  % ...
1578	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
1579	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
1580	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1581	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
1582	  % is an isomorphism.
1583	  % ...
1584	  % \begin{definition}
1585	  % \label{definition-pic}
1586	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1587	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the
1588	  % abelian group whose elements are isomorphism classes of
1589	  % invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, with addition
1590	  % corresponding to tensor product.
1591	  % \end{definition}"
1592	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1593	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
1594	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
1595	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under
1596	  tensor product).}
1597	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
1598	  bundle. The dual
1599	  \[
1600	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1601	  \]
1602	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
1603	  isomorphism
1604	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
1605	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
1606	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
1607	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
1608	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1609	\end{lemma}
1610	
1611	\begin{proof}
1612	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
1613	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1614	  Set \(L^{-1} := \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\).
1615	
1616	  \emph{Step 1 (\(L^{-1}\) is a line bundle).} Local triviality of \(L\)
1617	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\)) furnishes an open cover \(\{U\}\) of
1618	  \(X\) with trivialisations \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes
1619	  with restriction along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow X\), so on each \(U\)
1620	  \[
1621	    L^{-1}\big|_U \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1622	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1623	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U ,
1624	  \]
1625	  the last identification being the evaluation-at-\(1\) isomorphism for the dual of a
1626	  free rank-one module. Hence \(L^{-1}\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an
1627	  object of \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
1628	
1629	  \emph{Step 2 (the contraction is a local isomorphism).} The evaluation/contraction
1630	  morphism
1631	  \[
1632	    \varepsilon_L \;\colon\; L \otimes_X L^{-1}
1633	      \;=\; L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1634	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_X,
1635	    \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi(s),
1636	  \]
1637	  is a morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Restricting to a trivialising
1638	  open \(U\), the restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1639	  commutes \(\otimes_X\) past \((-)|_U\):
1640	  \[
1641	    (L \otimes_X L^{-1})\big|_U \;\cong\; L|_U \otimes_U L^{-1}|_U
1642	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U ,
1643	  \]
1644	  using the trivialisations of Step~1. Under these identifications \(\varepsilon_L|_U\)
1645	  becomes the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\) of
1646	  the free rank-one module with its dual, which is precisely the left unitor
1647	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\)) and hence an
1648	  isomorphism. Thus \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) is an isomorphism on each member of the cover.
1649	
1650	  \emph{Step 3 (glue to a global isomorphism).} The local trivialisations are induced
1651	  by the open immersions with no choices, so the local contraction isomorphisms
1652	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) agree on the overlaps \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1653	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the unitor under further restriction. As
1654	  ``being an isomorphism'' is local on \(X\) --- a morphism of sheaves of
1655	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose restriction to every member of an open cover is an
1656	  isomorphism is itself an isomorphism --- the globally-defined \(\varepsilon_L\) is an
1657	  isomorphism \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\). Equivalently, the
1658	  local inverses glue (they agree on overlaps for the same reason) to a two-sided
1659	  inverse of \(\varepsilon_L\).
1660	
1661	  Consequently every line bundle \(L\) has the two-sided tensor inverse \(L^{-1}\) under
1662	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes of line bundles is an abelian
1663	  group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1664	\end{proof}
1665	
1666	\begin{lemma}
1667	
1668	\leanok
1669	[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial carrier
1670	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
1671	  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
1673	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,
1674	        lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
1675	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
1676	  \emph{locally-trivial} subtype
1677	  \[
1678	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1679	    \;\;=\;\;
1680	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
1681	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M \,\bigr\}
1682	  \]
1683	  introduced in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. The bifunctor
1684	  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
1685	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
1686	  if \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M'\) then
1687	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\). Hence \(\otimes\)
1688	  closes on \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\), supplying the binary
1689	  operation the relative group law multiplies with.
1690	\end{lemma}
1691	
1692	\begin{proof}
1693	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
1694	  \leanok
1695	  Closure is exactly
1696	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}: the tensor product of two
1697	  locally-trivial rank-one modules is again locally trivial of rank one
1698	  (\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_isLocallyTrivial}\)). The Lean construction
1699	  \(\mathtt{tensorObjOnProduct}\) applies this directly to a pair of objects of
1700	  the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) subtype \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\),
1701	  returning the tensor with its locally-trivial witness; no
1702	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate and no coherence isomorphism enters this
1703	  closure statement. (The \(\otimes\)-invertibility carrier
1704	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible} is a
1705	  separate predicate, connected to \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) only by an
1706	  off-critical-path equivalence; the units-group packaging is
1707	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, not this lemma.)
1708	\end{proof}
1709	
1710	\begin{lemma}
1711	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
1712	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
1713	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.LineBundle.OnProduct.pullback_tensorObj_iso}
1714	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1715	  % NOTE: pullback preserves the structure sheaf and tensor products (and hence
1716	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
1717	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1718	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1719	  % restriction-compatibility iso.
1720	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
1721	  functor
1722	  \[
1723	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1724	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1725	  \]
1726	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1727	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1728	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1729	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1730	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1731	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1732	  canonical isomorphism
1733	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
1734	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
1735	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
1736	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
1737	\end{lemma}
1738	
1739	\begin{proof}
1740	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1741	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1742	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1743	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1744	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1745	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1746	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1747	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1748	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1749	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1750	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1751	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1752	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
1753	  same cover.
1754	\end{proof}
1755	
1756	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1757	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1758	
1759	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1760	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1761	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1762	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1763	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1764	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1765	design template. Under the route~(e) fallback the inverse of an invertible object
1766	is carried by the predicate itself, so neither the open-immersion
1767	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a
1768	separate tensor-inverse lemma is needed there. Under the PRIMARY locally-trivial
1769	route, by contrast, both \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (via the PRIMARY proof
1770	of \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}) and the tensor-inverse lemma
1771	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (supplying inverses to
1772	\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) are on the critical path.
1773	
1774	\begin{definition}\leanok
1775	[\(\otimes\)-invertibility of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module]
1776	  \label{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}
1777	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible}
1778	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1779	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
1780	  % modules), Definition 01CS (definition-invertible) + Lemma 0B8K
1781	  % (lemma-invertible, the "$\exists\,\mathcal{N}$" characterisation)
1782	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4079).
1783	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1784	  % "\begin{definition}
1785	  % \label{definition-invertible}
1786	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
1787	  % {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
1788	  % of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that
1789	  % the functor
1790	  % $$
1791	  % \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \longrightarrow \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X),\quad
1792	  % \mathcal{F} \longmapsto \mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}
1793	  % $$
1794	  % is an equivalence of categories. We say that $\mathcal{L}$ is
1795	  % {\it trivial} if it is isomorphic as an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
1796	  % to $\mathcal{O}_X$.
1797	  % \end{definition}
1798	  % [\dots]
1799	  % \begin{lemma}
1800	  % \label{lemma-invertible}
1801	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Let $\mathcal{L}$
1802	  % be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Equivalent are
1803	  % \begin{enumerate}
1804	  % \item $\mathcal{L}$ is invertible, and
1805	  % \item there exists an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{N}$
1806	  % such that
1807	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X$.
1808	  % \end{enumerate}
1809	  % In this case $\mathcal{L}$ is locally a direct summand of a finite free
1810	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-module and the module $\mathcal{N}$ in (2) is isomorphic to
1811	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
1812	  % \end{lemma}"
1813	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1814	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CS and Lemma~0B8K. An invertible
1815	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is one for which \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1816	  (-)\) is an equivalence; equivalently there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with
1817	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), and
1818	  then \(\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
1819	  \mathcal{O}_X)\).}

input.content:
# Blocker report: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`

**File:** `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (L1375)
**Iter:** 218 (PRIMARY critical-path objective)
**Verdict:** INFRASTRUCTURE MISSING — the INCOMPLETE gate of PROGRESS.md fired.
The informal agent was called (`--provider auto`, MOONSHOT key set) but returned
HTTP 401 *Invalid Authentication*, so no external sketch is available; the
analysis below is derived from the on-disk Mathlib API.

## Goal

```lean
lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
```

`X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`; `tensorObj` is the substrate tensor
(sheafification of `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); the unit is
`SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf = 𝒪_X`. `IsLocallyTrivial M` = for every
`x` an affine open `U ∋ x` with `M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`.

## Blueprint route (`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`) and where it bottoms out

The blueprint sets `Linv := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(L, 𝒪_X)` (the internal hom / dual),
shows it is locally trivial, and produces the contraction/evaluation
`ε_L : L ⊗_X Linv → 𝒪_X`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, which is a local iso (= left unitor on
the trivialising cover) and hence a global iso since "is-an-iso" is local.

**The construction is blocked at its FIRST step**, before any tactic state can be
advanced (one cannot even `refine ⟨Linv, ?_, ?_⟩` because `Linv` cannot be named):

1. **Missing primitive A — the internal hom object `ℋom_{𝒪_X}(L, 𝒪_X)` as a
   `SheafOfModules`.** Mathlib at `b80f227` has
   - NO `MonoidalClosed (SheafOfModules R)` instance,
   - NO `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)` instance (verified absent: `loogle`
     `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules ?R)` → no results; the file's §2 comment
     records the same "verified-absent `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`
     wall"),
   - NO `SheafOfModules`-level internal-hom / dual / `ihom` construction
     (`CategoryTheory.sheafHom` produces a `Sheaf J (Type …)`, NOT a
     `SheafOfModules`; it carries no `𝒪_X`-module structure).

   So the dual object `Linv` has no `Scheme.Modules`-level construction.

2. **Missing primitive B — the evaluation/contraction morphism**
   `L ⊗_X ℋom(L,𝒪_X) → 𝒪_X` is the counit of the (absent) internal-hom
   adjunction; it has no construction either.

3. **No monoidal-category escape hatch.** The standard categorical route
   ("an invertible object of a monoidal category has a two-sided inverse",
   e.g. dualizable/rigid-object API) is unavailable *by design*: the project
   deliberately does **not** build `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` for the varying
   structure sheaf (see `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path` and memory
   `commring-pic-is-skeleton-route`). So there is no monoidal structure to read an
   inverse off of.

4. **No object-gluing/descent fallback.** The alternative — glue a global `Linv`
   from local `𝒪_{U_i}` pieces along transition functions `g_{ij}^{-1}` — needs
   *object-level descent* for `SheafOfModules` (build a global object from a cover +
   cocycle). Mathlib has section-level gluing (`TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing`)
   and the "iso is local" fact (`MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.IsLocalAtTarget` for
   subcanonical `J`, `Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`), but **no construction
   that assembles a global `SheafOfModules` object from local objects + transition
   isos**. So the gluing route is also Mathlib-absent.

## Exact missing ingredient (for `mathlib-build` / `mathlib-analogist`)

Either of the following would unblock the PRIMARY; **(I) is the blueprint's route**:

- **(I) Internal hom / dual for `SheafOfModules R`** (or for `PresheafOfModules R`
  then sheafify, mirroring how `tensorObj` sheafifies the presheaf tensor):
  a functor `ℋom : (SheafOfModules R)ᵒᵖ × SheafOfModules R ⥤ SheafOfModules R`
  (at least its action `M ↦ ℋom(M, 𝒪_X)` and the evaluation
  `M ⊗ ℋom(M,𝒪_X) → 𝒪_X`), i.e. a `MonoidalClosed` structure or a bespoke dual.
  Statement of the precise object needed:
  ```
  def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
      X.Modules                                   -- ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)
  def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.eval {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
      tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf   -- s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)
  ```
  Then: `dual` of a locally-trivial `M` is locally trivial (internal hom commutes
  with open-immersion restriction + dual of free rank-one is free rank-one), and
  `eval` is a local iso (= contraction `𝒪_U ⊗ 𝒪_U ≅ 𝒪_U`, the left unitor) hence
  a global iso by `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (CLOSED) + "iso is local".

- **(II) Object-level descent for `SheafOfModules`:** a construction taking an open
  cover `{U_i}` of `X`, objects `M_i : (U_i).Modules`, and transition isomorphisms
  `M_i|_{U_i ∩ U_j} ≅ M_j|_{U_i ∩ U_j}` satisfying the cocycle condition, and
  producing a global `M : X.Modules` with `M|_{U_i} ≅ M_i`. With `M_i = 𝒪_{U_i}`
  and `g_{ij}^{-1}` transitions this builds `Linv` directly.

## Downstream steps that ARE available once `Linv`/`eval` exist

`tensorObj_restrict_iso` (CLOSED, iter-217), `tensorObj_unit_iso`,
`restrictIsoUnitOfLE`, and `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (CLOSED) already supply the
local-triviality of `Linv` and the local-iso-⟹-global-iso bookkeeping (mirror the
existing `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proof at L1349). The ONLY genuinely absent
pieces are A and B (the dual object and its evaluation).

## Recommendation

Assign primitive (I) [`SheafOfModules` internal-hom/dual + evaluation] in
`mathlib-build` mode (it is the blueprint's route and the smaller of the two; the
presheaf-level internal hom + sheafification mirrors the existing `tensorObj`
build). Per the progress-critic ts218 PRE-CAUTION, this is the mathlib-analogist
trigger to run before iter-220 — do NOT push a `dual`-shaped helper-sorry into
`exists_tensorObj_inverse` (the iter-214 d.1 anti-pattern).

input.content:
# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean — iter-218

## PRIMARY: `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (was L1375, now L1390)

### Attempt 1 — blueprint dual route
- **Approach:** `lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`: set `Linv := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(L, 𝒪_X)`,
  show it locally trivial, exhibit the contraction `L ⊗_X Linv → 𝒪_X` and prove it a
  global iso via the CLOSED `tensorObj_restrict_iso` + `tensorObj_unit_iso`, mirroring
  `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (L1349).
- **Result:** INFRASTRUCTURE MISSING (INCOMPLETE gate fired). Blocked at step 1 — the
  dual object `Linv` cannot even be **named**, so no tactic state advances.
- **Exact blocker (verified against on-disk Mathlib `b80f227`):**
  1. No `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)` instance — `loogle`
     `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules ?R)` → no results (matches the file's §2
     "verified-absent `MonoidalClosed` wall" note).
  2. No `MonoidalClosed (SheafOfModules R)` / no `SheafOfModules`-level internal-hom,
     dual, or evaluation object. `CategoryTheory.sheafHom` lands in `Sheaf J (Type …)`,
     which carries NO `𝒪_X`-module structure, so it is not the internal hom of
     `𝒪_X`-modules.
  3. No monoidal escape hatch: `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` is deliberately NOT built
     for the varying structure sheaf (`rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`, memory
     `commring-pic-is-skeleton-route`), so dualizable/rigid-object API is unavailable.
  4. No object-level descent for `SheafOfModules` (build a global object from a cover +
     transition cocycle); Mathlib has section-gluing (`existsUnique_gluing`) and "iso is
     local" (`MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.IsLocalAtTarget` for subcanonical `J`,
     `Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`) but NOT object assembly.
- **Informal agent:** called (`--provider auto`, MOONSHOT key set) → HTTP 401 Invalid
  Authentication. No external sketch obtained; blocker analysis is Mathlib-source-derived.
- **Missing primitive (mathlib-analogist target):** an internal-hom/dual + evaluation for
  `SheafOfModules R` (preferably presheaf-level then sheafify, mirroring `tensorObj`):
  `dual M : X.Modules` and `eval : tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`.
  Full decomposition + the available downstream steps written to
  `informal/exists_tensorObj_inverse.md`.
- **Gate compliance:** per the progress-critic ts218 PRE-CAUTION + PROGRESS.md INCOMPLETE
  gate, did NOT push a `dual`-shaped helper-sorry forward (iter-214 d.1 anti-pattern).
  Left the typed `sorry` with a detailed in-code blocker comment; updated the stale
  "iter-202 scaffold" docstring to the iter-218 blocker analysis.

## SECONDARY (bonus −1): re-route `tensorObj_assoc_iso` + delete whiskering apparatus

- **Status of `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (L1152→L1150):** ALREADY a complete (no-sorry) body via
  the ROUTE-(d) whiskering composite. It transitively depends on the L600 sorry
  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` through `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`/`W_whiskerRight_of_W`
  (the live `_of_W` chain). So the −1 = eliminate the L600 sorry.
- **Two paths to the −1, BOTH Mathlib-blocked:**
  - **(a) Close L600 directly.** Needs the stalkwise route: (d.1-bridge) site-level `J.W` ⇔
    topological stalkwise-iso on `Opens X`, and (d.2) stalk-⊗ commutation
    `(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` over the varying ring. d.2 is Mathlib-absent (the
    largest piece); the progress-critic explicitly warned against pushing this
    (iter-214 d.1 anti-pattern). A stalk-free route was considered ("g ∈ J.W ⇒ locally an
    iso ⇒ F ◁ g locally iso") but local bijectivity is per-section-covering-sieve, not a
    uniform cover where `g` restricts to a presheaf iso, so it does not avoid stalks; the
    categorical `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is circular (it IS what L600 would supply).
  - **(b) Re-route assoc onto `tensorObj_restrict_iso` + delete the apparatus.** Needs to
    build a GLOBAL iso by gluing local isos over a cover (the "glue via Hom-is-a-sheaf"
    step) — i.e. morphism-level descent for `SheafOfModules`. The local isos
    (`tensorObj_restrict_iso` twice + presheaf associator) do NOT auto-assemble into a
    global morphism; that needs the sheaf-Hom descent construction, which is heavy/absent
    at this level. The current whiskering proof bridges the inner sheafification
    (`a(a(M⊗N).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M⊗N)⊗P.val)`) precisely via the whiskered unit — the
    `restrict_iso` route cannot reproduce that bridge without gluing.
- **Decision:** did NOT attempt a broken partial re-route (it would leave non-compiling
  gluing scaffolding); reported the precise blocker instead.
- **Dead-code audit (for the eventual deletion package):** `lean_references` confirms
  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (L444) is referenced only by `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`
  (L521→523), and `stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso` (L799) has **zero** references. The `_of_flat`
  chain (`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat`, `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`,
  `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`) and the stalk lemmas (`stalkLinearMap`, `_germ`,
  `_bijective_of_isIso`, `stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso`) are effectively dead. **NOT deleted**
  this iter: deleting them removes NO sorry (the L600 sorry lives in the LIVE `_of_W`
  chain used by `tensorObj_assoc_iso`), so deletion gives zero sorry-progress and only
  risks the build; it belongs in the same package as the assoc re-route once (b) is
  unblocked.

## Ride-along cleanup (done; build kept green)
- Fixed false "typed `sorry`" docstrings on `tensorObj` (L981) and `tensorObj_functoriality`
  (L997) — both are fully defined now.
- Updated the stale "iter-202 scaffold" tail of the `exists_tensorObj_inverse` docstring to
  the iter-218 INCOMPLETE-gate blocker analysis.
- **Did NOT** drop `@[implicit_reducible]` on `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (directive item):
  it is a `def` of class type `AddCommGroup`, so dropping the attribute triggers the
  "class type must be marked `@[reducible]`/`@[implicit_reducible]`" linter and adds a
  warning. Retained it (with a note) to keep the build clean; recorded the reason in-code.
- **Did NOT** touch the 17 deprecated `Sheaf.val` sites: these are defeq-load-bearing for
  the assoc-iso carrier bridge (`Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf` is only `rfl`-defeq, not
  syntactically equal, to `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …`), so a mechanical replacement risks
  breaking `rfl`/`exact` defeq closes. Left as-is (deprecation warnings only).

## Summary
- **Sorry count: 3 → 3** (unchanged). Open: `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (L600),
  `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L1390), `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (L1440, out of scope this iter).
- **Closed:** none.
- **Still open + why:** `exists_tensorObj_inverse` — Mathlib-absent internal-hom/dual for
  `SheafOfModules` (blocked at step 1, cannot name `Linv`). L600 — Mathlib-absent stalk d.2 /
  morphism-descent. Both reported precisely; `exists_tensorObj_inverse` blocker → `informal/`.
- **Adjacent sorries:** examined both other sorries (L600, L1440); both blocked on the same
  missing-descent/stalk infrastructure family, not closable this iter.

## Why I stopped
- **Infrastructure missing** (PRIMARY `exists_tensorObj_inverse`): the dual object
  `ℋom_{𝒪_X}(L,𝒪_X)` and its evaluation have NO `SheafOfModules`-level construction at
  `b80f227` (no `MonoidalClosed` on `PresheafOfModules`/`SheafOfModules`, no internal hom,
  no object-level descent; no `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` by design). The construction is
  blocked at its first step, so no tactic progress (not even `refine ⟨Linv, …⟩`) is possible
  without pushing a forbidden `dual`-shaped helper-sorry. Informal agent attempted, returned
  401. Exact missing primitive + decomposition in `informal/exists_tensorObj_inverse.md`.
  This is precisely the mathlib-analogist trigger the progress-critic ts218 PRE-CAUTION
  scheduled for before iter-220.
- **Code progress this iter:** none on sorry count (honest). Changes are docstring
  corrections (cosmetic) + the new `informal/` blocker report + a precise dead-code/blocker
  audit for the SECONDARY package. No working proof was within reach without the named
  absent infrastructure.
